reason: The snippet `def load_config(path, defaults={}):` is confirmed at RIGHT line 4 of the diff; the mutable default argument is a real Python anti-pattern with a concrete future-maintainer risk and a clear fix.
comment: Mutable default argument: `defaults={}` is evaluated once at function definition time and shared across all calls. Although the current code only reads from `defaults` (never writes to it), the pattern is a well-known Python trap — a future maintainer adding `defaults[k] = ...` inside this function would silently corrupt the shared default for every subsequent call. Use `defaults=None` and `if defaults is None: defaults = {}` instead.
